title: IMD Amaravati: Hot, humid conditions likely in Coastal AP, Rayalaseema - News9live
full_text:
IMD Amaravati: Hot, humid conditions likely in Coastal AP, Rayalaseema
Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Agency has said that heatwave conditions will prevail in 51 mandals on Monday and in 57 districts on Tuesday.
Visakhapatnam: Hot and humid conditions are likely to prevail at some places in Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Yanam and Rayalaseema over the next seven days, India Meteorological Department in Amaravati said.
Gradual rise in maximum temperatures by 2-3 degrees Celsius is likely over the next few days. Lower tropospheric southerlies/ south-westerly winds prevail over AP and Yanam.
APSDMA warns of heatwave:
Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Agency (APSDMA) MD Prakhar Jain has said that heatwave conditions will prevail in 51 mandals on Monday and in 57 districts on Tuesday.
He further said that on Monday, severe heatwaves are likely to occur in 9 mandals of Srikakulam district, 18 mandals of Vizianagaram district, 15 in Parvathipuram Manyam district, 1 in Alluri district, 3 in Polavaram, 3 in Anakapalli, and 2 in Eluru district. Additionally, he stated that heatwaves are expected to impact 2 mandals in Srikakulam district, 5 in Vizianagaram district, 4 in Alluri district, 9 in Polavaram, 5 in Anakapalli, 5 in Kakinada, 9 in East Godavari, 4 in Eluru, 3 in NTR district, 2 in Palnadu, and 1 in Nandyal district.
On Sunday, Kadapa district recorded the highest temperature of 43.8 degrees Celsius followed by 43.1 degrees in Varadayyapalem (Tirupati district), 42.9 degrees in Nandyal, 42.3 degrees in Therannapalle Anantapur district.

title: Severe heatwave warning issued for Andhra Pradesh; 51 mandals at risk - Mathrubhumi English
search_term: heatwave (Anantapur OR Chittoor OR "East Godavari" OR Guntur OR Krishna OR Kurnool OR Nellore OR Prakasam OR Srikakulam OR Visakhapatnam OR Vizianagaram OR "West Godavari" OR "YSR Kadapa") Andhra Pradesh
full_text:
The impact is mainly forecast across north coastal districts, with high temperatures also likely in coastal and Rayalaseema regions
Amaravati: The Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority (APSDMA) has warned of severe heatwave conditions across several parts of the state on Monday.
According to the APSDMA, severe heatwaves are likely to affect 51 mandals, while heatwave conditions are expected in another 49 mandals. The impact is mainly forecast across north coastal districts, with high temperatures also likely in coastal and Rayalaseema regions.
APSDMA Managing Director Prakhar Jain said in an official statement that “severe heatwave conditions are likely to prevail across several parts of the state on Monday.”
He further added that on Tuesday, severe heatwaves are expected in 53 mandals, while 57 mandals may experience heatwave conditions.

On Monday, severe heatwaves are likely in Srikakulam (9 mandals), Vizianagaram (18), Parvathipuram Manyam (15), Alluri Sitarama Raju (1), Polavaram (3), Anakapalli (3) and Eluru (2). Heatwave conditions are also expected in Srikakulam (2 mandals), Vizianagaram (5), Alluri Sitarama Raju (4), Polavaram (9), Anakapalli (5), Kakinada (5), East Godavari (9), Eluru (4), NTR (3), Palnadu (2) and Nandyal (1).
Jain noted that on Sunday, the highest temperature recorded in the state was 43.8°C in Pottipadu in Kadapa district. Other high readings included 43.1°C in Varadaiahpalem (Tirupati district), 42.9°C in Nandyal, and 42.3°C in Terannapalle in Anantapur district.
The APSDMA has urged people to take necessary precautions, including avoiding direct exposure to sunlight and staying well hydrated.
Here are 10 essential safety tips to stay cool and safe during a heatwave:
- Stay well hydrated: Drink water regularly throughout the day, even if you don’t feel thirsty. Keep a bottle with you at all times.
- Eat water-rich foods: Include fruits and vegetables like watermelon, cucumber, oranges and celery to help maintain hydration.
- Keep your home cool: Close curtains, blinds or shutters during the day to block sunlight, and open windows at night for cooler air.
- Wear light clothing: Choose loose-fitting, light-coloured cotton or linen to help your body stay cool.
- Cool key pulse points: Use a damp cloth or cold water on wrists, neck and temples to quickly reduce body temperature.
- Avoid peak heat hours: Stay indoors between 11:00 AM and 4:00 PM. If you go out, stay in shaded areas.
- Avoid heat-generating appliances: Limit use of ovens and stovetops; opt for cold or no-cook meals like salads and sandwiches.
- Take lukewarm showers: Lukewarm water helps regulate body temperature more effectively than very cold showers.
- Limit caffeine and alcohol: Both can contribute to dehydration, so reduce intake during extreme heat.
- Check on vulnerable people: Keep in touch with elderly people, children and pets who are more at risk during heatwaves.

title: Heat Intensity Rising Across Andhra Pradesh; 44.4°C Recorded in Kadapa - Deccan Chronicle
search_term: heatwave (Anantapur OR Chittoor OR "East Godavari" OR Guntur OR Krishna OR Kurnool OR Nellore OR Prakasam OR Srikakulam OR Visakhapatnam OR Vizianagaram OR "West Godavari" OR "YSR Kadapa") Andhra Pradesh
full_text:
Authorities advised the public to take precautions amid rising temperatures.
VISAKHAPATNAM: The intensity of heat is gradually increasing across Andhra Pradesh, with Kadapa recording the highest temperature of 44.4 degrees Celsius on Monday, Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority (APSDMA) managing director Prakhar Jain said.
He said heatwave conditions are likely to intensify further on Tuesday in Rayalaseema and coastal districts.
According to the forecast, severe heatwave conditions are expected in 33 mandals across Srikakulam, Vizianagaram, Parvathipuram Manyam, Alluri Sitharama Raju, Polavaram and Eluru districts.
In addition, heatwave impact is likely in 44 mandals across Srikakulam, Vizianagaram, Alluri Sitharama Raju, Polavaram, Anakapalli, Kakinada, East Godavari, Eluru, NTR, Nellore and Tirupati districts.
On Monday, Kadapa recorded 44.4°C followed by Sanjamala in Nandyal (44°C), Varadaiahpalem in Tirupati (43.8°C), Anumalapalli in Markapuram (43.2°C), Gudur in Kurnool (43°C), Durgi in Palnadu (42.8°C), Rayadurg in Anantapur (42.7°C), Kommi Padu in Nellore (42.4°C), Addanki in Prakasam and Talupula in Sri Sathya Sai (42.3°C), Nooliveedu in Annamayya (42°C), Tavanampalle in Chittoor (41.9°C) and Kanchikacherla in NTR district (41.4°C).
Authorities advised the public to take precautions amid rising temperatures.

title: Severe heat wave conditions likely across mandals in state - The Hans India
search_term: heatwave (Anantapur OR Chittoor OR "East Godavari" OR Guntur OR Krishna OR Kurnool OR Nellore OR Prakasam OR Srikakulam OR Visakhapatnam OR Vizianagaram OR "West Godavari" OR "YSR Kadapa") Andhra Pradesh
full_text:
Amaravati: Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority Managing Director Prakhar Jain has warned that severe heat wave conditions are expected across several mandals in the State over the next two days.
On Monday severe heat wave conditions are likely to prevail in 51 mandals, while 49 mandals may experience moderate heatwave conditions. On Tuesday, the intensity is expected to increase, with 53 mandals facing severe heatwaves and 57 mandals experiencing heatwave conditions.
Prakhar Jain stated that North Coastal Andhra districts are likely to experience strong heat wave winds, while Coastal Andhra and Rayalaseema districts may record very high maximum temperatures. People have been advised to take necessary precautions to protect themselves from the intense heat. According to the forecast, on Monday, severe heat waves are expected in several mandals across districts such as Srikakulam, Vizianagaram, Parvathipuram Manyam, Alluri Sitharama Raju, Polavaram, Anakapalli, and Eluru. In addition, heatwave conditions are likely in mandals spread across Srikakulam, Vizianagaram, Alluri Sitharama Raju, Polavaram, Anakapalli, Kakinada, East Godavari, Eluru, NTR, Palnadu, and Nandyal districts.
On Sunday, extremely high temperatures were recorded in several locations across the state. The highest temperature of 43.8°C was recorded at Pottipadu in Kadapa district, followed by 43.1°C at Varadaiahpalem in Tirupati district and 42.9°C in Nandyal. Other areas such as Anantapur, Kurnool, Palnadu, Sri Sathya Sai, Markapuram, Nellore, and West Godavari also recorded temperatures above 41°C.

title: APSDMA Warns of High Temperatures, Heatwave Conditions Across AP - Dailyhunt
search_term: heatwave (Anantapur OR Chittoor OR "East Godavari" OR Guntur OR Krishna OR Kurnool OR Nellore OR Prakasam OR Srikakulam OR Visakhapatnam OR Vizianagaram OR "West Godavari" OR "YSR Kadapa") Andhra Pradesh
full_text:
Amaravati: The APSDMA warned of high temperatures and heatwave conditions across several parts of Andhra Pradesh on Sunday.
High temperatures are likely to prevail in Krishna, NTR, Palnadu, Markapuram, Prakasam, Nellore, Kurnool, Nandyal, Anantapur, Sri Sathya Sai, Kadapa, Annamayya, Chittoor and Tirupati districts, while heatwave conditions are expected in north coastal districts, Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority (APSDMA) Managing Director Prakhar Jain said in an official release.
He further said that severe heatwave conditions are likely in 19 mandals, while 35 mandals may experience heatwave impact on Sunday. He noted that heat intensity has been rising across the state, with temperature exceeding 40 degrees Celsius recorded in 200 mandals on April 11.On Saturday, the highest temperature recorded were 44.7 degrees Celsius in Pottipadu in Kadapa district, 44.3 degrees in Kommipadu in Nellore district, 44.2 degrees in Varadayyapalem in Tirupati district, and 42.8 degrees in Gospadu in Nandyal district and Kopperapadu in Prakasam district. Similarly, heatwave conditions are expected to prevail in a total of 70 mandals on Monday, he added. The APSDMA advised people to remain alert to heatwaves and humid conditions.
